title: podman pull ubuntu fails on macOS
body:
### Issue Description

```
❯ podman pull ubuntu                   
Resolved "ubuntu" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest...
Error: copying system image from manifest list: parsing image configuration: Get "https://docker-images-prod.6aa30f8b08e16409b46e0173d6de2f56.r2.cloudflarestorage.com/registry-v2/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/c3/c3d1a34325805c22bf44a5157224bcff58dc6a8868558c7746d6a2ea64eb191c/data?X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&X-Amz-Credential=f1baa2dd9b876aeb89efebbfc9e5d5f4%2F20250209%2Fauto%2Fs3%2Faws4_request&X-Amz-Date=20250209T123950Z&X-Amz-Expires=1200&X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&X-Amz-Signature=e7e66bb0b3ee0af4eac98147655dd7542818a5224fc385db28367de21723d9ef": tls: failed to verify certificate: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
```

```
❯ brew info podman
==> podman: stable 5.3.2 (bottled), HEAD
Tool for managing OCI containers and pods
https://podman.io/
Installed
/opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/5.3.2_1 (201 files, 77.5MB) *
  Poured from bottle using the formulae.brew.sh API on 2025-02-09 at 13:24:04
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/p/podman.rb
License: Apache-2.0 AND GPL-3.0-or-later
==> Dependencies
Build: go, go-md2man, make
==> Requirements
Required: macOS >= 13 (or Linux)
==> Options
--HEAD
	Install HEAD version
==> Caveats
In order to run containers locally, podman depends on a Linux kernel.
One can be started manually using `podman machine` from this package.
To start a podman VM automatically at login, also install the cask
"podman-desktop".

zsh completions have been installed to:
  /opt/homebrew/share/zsh/site-functions
==> Analytics
install: 16,768 (30 days), 38,905 (90 days), 181,255 (365 days)
install-on-request: 15,612 (30 days), 36,399 (90 days), 162,865 (365 days)
build-error: 12 (30 days)
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue


1. `brew install podman`
2. `podman machine init`
3. `podman machine start`
4. `podman pull ubuntu`


### Describe the results you received

Cannot pull or run images because of an error `failed to verify certificate:`.

### Describe the results you expected

Pull and run ubuntu, alpine, or any other container.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

macOS, installed view brew

### Additional information

For some strange reason busybox sometimes works and sometimes not. When I tried alpine for the first time it worked, but now does not. Ubuntu consistently does not work.

I tried to completely remove it and reinstall a few times, but it does not help.
```
podman machine stop
podman machine reset
brew remove podman
rm -f ~/.config/containers
# reboot
```

comments.body:
Now I am getting 

```
❯ podman machine stop 
Error: unable to clean up gvproxy: process 3282 has not ended
```
Are you behind a proxy that intercepts all https traffic? If so you have to add the certs to your machine

I found out that my mac has a company installed security software that inspects all network traffic. It added extra root certificates to the macOS Keychain Access. Exporting those and adding them to the podman machine as described in the [docs](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman-install-certificate-authority.md) solved the problem.

Is there a way that podman could automatically use the host OS key store?

We already copy certs from certain locations
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/1d06a1f8d5c4a2d36e8792873a308de881b05891/pkg/machine/ignition/ignition.go#L410-L447
All certificates in the macOS keychain can be exported via `security find-certificate -a -p > allcerts.pem` command.
